high_level_intent.core_purpose: Turn self-erasure and helplessness into a still-moving survival anthem.
high_level_intent.emotional_engine: The song weaponizes a bright A-major pop-rock frame against severely negative self-talk, producing a smiling-but-desperate emotional burn rather than simple sadness.

contrast_device.primary: bright melodic optimism versus despairing lyric content
contrast_device.secondary: light acoustic verse texture versus explosive rock chorus release

high_level_intent.narrative_stance: A timid first-person narrator who never becomes fully heroic, but keeps choosing motion anyway.

suno_relevance.best_use_case: an anthemic J-pop song that sounds uplifting on the surface while carrying self-loathing, resignation, and stubborn will underneath
must_preserve: repeated degradation phrase that later flips into a life-affirming phrase
must_avoid: EDM-drop framing that removes the human struggle

section_intent.function: Establish a powerless witness who looks away from violence and shame.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Acoustic strumming keeps the track deceptively buoyant so the lyric weakness lands with more irony.
section_intent.melodic_role: bright melody used as emotional misdirection

section_intent.function: Escalate from passive observation to coerced complicity.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Keep the same light pop-rock frame so the uglier imagery feels even more disturbing.
section_intent.melodic_role: parallel to verse 1 for moral deepening rather than melodic novelty
section_intent.rhetorical_pattern: social pressure -> forced participation -> inner rot

section_intent.function: Compress the song into a breath-held collapse before the first release.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Strip back the groove, bring in pad and arpeggiated texture, and make the listener lean in.
section_intent.rhetorical_pattern: hope loss -> qualification failure -> fear of being unloved

section_intent.function: Convert weakness into a rough, shouted survival vow without solving the world's cruelty.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Full-band rock impact, distorted guitars, and maximum drum commitment.
section_intent.rhetorical_pattern: rejection of shame -> stubborn self-affirmation -> cruel world observation -> action vow
section_intent.hook_logic: The title line lands not as surrender but as a statement of cosmic insignificance that still cannot stop action.

section_intent.function: Push the self-worth collapse to its most humiliating social form.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Return to the earlier acoustic-led brightness so the loss feels cyclical rather than resolved.
section_intent.melodic_role: familiar verse shape used for deeper defeat

section_intent.function: Turn doubt into a philosophical crisis about belief, fraud, and salvation.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Repeat the breakdown grammar so the listener recognizes this as another cliff edge.

section_intent.function: Shift from despair to angry realism and build toward the final conversion.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Reintroduce the pulse and let the band push forward without spending the full chorus energy yet.
section_intent.rhetorical_pattern: defiant outburst -> reality check -> action vow with harsher cost

section_intent.function: Flip the repeated decay mantra into a life-light mantra.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Put the earlier chant mechanism on top of a peak-band texture so the reversal feels earned and physical.
section_intent.melodic_role: same structural engine as earlier repetition, but emotionally inverted
section_intent.rhetorical_pattern: repeated collapse phrase becomes repeated illumination phrase

section_intent.function: Restate the survival vow with expanded generosity and worse imagined circumstances.
section_intent.arrangement_intent: Keep the band fully open and let the repeated ending phrase feel unresolved on purpose.
section_intent.melodic_role: same chorus engine, greater emotional reach
section_intent.rhetorical_pattern: chorus return -> expanded wish for kindness -> even-if clauses -> repeated vow loop
section_intent.ending_effect: The looped ending should feel less like triumph and more like a refusal to stop.

production_palette: cheerful acoustic guitar strumming in verses
supportive bass and drums with clean forward pulse
pad and electric arpeggio during the pre-chorus compression
distorted guitar and full-rock chorus expansion
vocal_behavior: bridge delivery that rises into argument rather than sadness
lyric_behavior: repeated degrading phrase that mutates into a life-affirming phrase
defiant colloquial outburst as a chorus ignition point
distilled_conditioning.style_prompt_seed: Bright major-key J-pop rock at 134 BPM with cheerful acoustic-guitar strumming in the verses, a stripped pre-chorus with synth pad and electric arpeggio, then a full distorted-guitar chorus that sounds uplifting while the lyrics stay desperate. Keep the emotional engine built on contradiction: self-erasure, social pressure, and worthlessness pushed through a catchy, hopeful melodic frame. Let the bridge harden into angry realism, then flip the repeated decay mantra into a life-light climax before a final chorus that keeps repeating the action vow instead of giving clean triumph.
negative_prompt_hints: no glossy EDM drop replacing the rock chorus
no sentimental resolution that erases the bitterness

notes: This dossier is intentionally non-verbatim. It stores reusable compositional intent, not full lyric text.
The most important lesson is not the exact wording but the bright-versus-broken contradiction and the repeated phrase reversal.
